verses.1: Bless the LORD, my soul! LORD my God, You are very great; You are clothed with splendor and majesty,
verses.2: Covering Yourself with light as with a cloak, Stretching out heaven like a tent curtain.
verses.3: He lays the beams of His upper chambers in the waters; He makes the clouds His chariot; He walks on the wings of the wind;
verses.4: He makes the winds His messengers, Flaming fire His ministers.
verses.5: He established the earth upon its foundations, So that it will not totter forever and ever.
verses.6: You covered it with the deep sea as with a garment; The waters were standing above the mountains.
verses.7: They fled from Your rebuke, At the sound of Your thunder they hurried away.
verses.8: The mountains rose; the valleys sank down To the place which You established for them.
verses.9: You set a boundary so that they will not pass over, So that they will not return to cover the earth.
verses.10: He sends forth springs in the valleys; They flow between the mountains;
verses.11: They give drink to every animal of the field; The wild donkeys quench their thirst.
verses.12: The birds of the sky dwell beside them; They lift up their voices from among the branches.
verses.13: He waters the mountains from His upper chambers; The earth is satisfied with the fruit of His works.
verses.14: He causes the grass to grow for the cattle, And vegetation for the labor of mankind, So that they may produce food from the earth,
verses.15: And wine, which makes a human heart cheerful, So that he makes his face gleam with oil, And food, which sustains a human heart.
verses.16: The trees of the LORD drink their fill, The cedars of Lebanon which He planted,
verses.17: Where the birds build their nests, And the stork, whose home is the juniper trees.
verses.18: The high mountains are for the wild goats; The cliffs are a refuge for the rock hyrax.
verses.19: He made the moon for the seasons; The sun knows the place of its setting.
verses.20: You appoint darkness and it becomes night, In which all the animals of the forest prowl about.
verses.21: The young lions roar for their prey And seek their food from God.
verses.22: When the sun rises they withdraw, And they lie down in their dens.
verses.23: A person goes out to his work And to his labor until evening.
verses.24: LORD, how many are Your works! In wisdom You have made them all; The earth is full of Your possessions.
verses.25: There is the sea, great and broad, In which are swarms without number, Animals both small and great.
verses.26: The ships move along there, And Leviathan, which You have formed to have fun in it.
verses.27: They all wait for You To give them their food in due season.
verses.28: You give to them, they gather it up; You open Your hand, they are satisfied with good.
verses.29: You hide Your face, they are terrified; You take away their breath, they perish And return to their dust.
verses.30: You send forth Your Spirit, they are created; And You renew the face of the ground.
verses.31: May the glory of the LORD endure forever; May the LORD rejoice in His works;
verses.32: He looks at the earth, and it trembles; He touches the mountains, and they smoke.
verses.33: I will sing to the LORD as long as I live; I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.
verses.34: May my praise be pleasing to Him; As for me, I shall rejoice in the LORD.
verses.35: May sinners be removed from the earth And may the wicked be no more. Bless the LORD, my soul. Praise the LORD!
